claim: Expert-rubric judges showed stronger reported inter-judge agreement than the generic-rubric comparison.
scope: 92 Figma runs reported in rubric comparison
caveat: Comparison changed author source rubric count grouping weights anchor language and observability together
decision_use: supporting: indicates promise for scoring consistency; not causal isolation

claim: Generic-rubric judges had lower reported inter-judge agreement.
caveat: Does not isolate expert authorship as the cause
decision_use: supporting comparator: establishes the reported consistency gap while limiting attribution

claim: Expert-rubric judges showed lower reported judge variance.
decision_use: supporting: indicates consistency; disconfirming for any inference from consistency to validity

claim: Generic-rubric judges showed higher reported judge variance.
decision_use: supporting comparator: establishes the reported variance gap; does not establish validity

claim: Automated fine-grained output grades weakly matched human judgments.
caveat: One Figma domain expert limits human-baseline generality
decision_use: disconfirming: weak observed human concordance blocks immediate adoption

claim: Automated fine-grained skill grades weakly matched human judgments.
caveat: No independent human process annotation validates the skill judges
decision_use: disconfirming: skill-level validity is not established

claim: Skill execution effects were directional and heterogeneous in a small ablation.
scope: Claude Codex and Gemini harness/model systems
caveat: Sample is underpowered and harness/model/integration factors remain bundled
decision_use: mixed: motivates a controlled pilot but cannot support a causal adoption claim

claim: One configured system had a large reported directional gain with skills.
scope: Subset of the 7 paired Figma runs
caveat: Too few runs for a stable causal adoption claim
decision_use: supporting signal: candidate effect to replicate; disconfirming for immediate causal adoption

claim: Some no-skill runs deployed, but the deployment difference is causally ambiguous.
caveat: Deployment difference alone cannot separate procedure content from integration and stochasticity
decision_use: mixed: operational signal requiring randomized controls and repeated runs

claim: Pairwise preference did not imply expert-threshold readiness.
scope: 31 rated Figma tasks with one domain expert
caveat: Single-expert threshold is not a universal professional standard
decision_use: disconfirming: relative wins are insufficient as an adoption gate
